Step 2 — Exploratory Data Analysis

2026-06-03

Sample

N = 7

d |>
  count(condition) |>
  mutate(pct = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 1)) |>
  kable(col.names = c("Condition", "N", "%"))
Condition N %
control 5 71.4
relevance 1 14.3
action 1 14.3 
d |>
  count(condition, variant_seen) |>
  kable(col.names = c("Condition", "Variant", "N"))
Condition Variant N
control control 3
control NA 2
relevance relevance2 1
action action1 1

Means and SDs by condition

map_dfr(outcome_vars, function(var) {
  d |>
    group_by(condition) |>
    summarise(
      M  = round(mean(.data[[var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2),
      SD = round(sd(.data[[var]],   na.rm = TRUE), 2),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) |>
    mutate(Outcome = outcome_labels[[var]])
}) |>
  select(Outcome, condition, M, SD) |>
  arrange(Outcome, condition) |>
  kable()
Outcome condition M SD
Article agreement (1) control NaN NA
Article agreement (1) relevance NaN NA
Article agreement (1) action NaN NA
Article agreement (2) control NaN NA
Article agreement (2) relevance NaN NA
Article agreement (2) action NaN NA

Behavioral intention (1) relevance NaN NA

Behavioral intention (2) relevance NaN NA
Behavioral intention (2) action NaN NA

Behavioral intention (3) relevance NaN NA
Behavioral intention (3) action NaN NA
Climate belief (1) control NaN NA
Climate belief (1) relevance NaN NA
Climate belief (1) action NaN NA
Climate belief (2) control NaN NA
Climate belief (2) relevance NaN NA
Climate belief (2) action NaN NA
Climate belief (3a) control NaN NA
Climate belief (3a) relevance NaN NA
Climate belief (3a) action NaN NA

Climate belief (3b) relevance NaN NA
Climate belief (3b) action NaN NA
Climate belief (3c) control NaN NA
Climate belief (3c) relevance NaN NA
Climate belief (3c) action NaN NA

Disapprove of funding cuts relevance NaN NA
Disapprove of funding cuts action NaN NA
Govt funding (general) control NaN NA
Govt funding (general) relevance NaN NA
Govt funding (general) action NaN NA
Share directly control 38 15.56
Share directly relevance 59 NA
Share directly action 53 NA
Share on social media control 41 35.36
Share on social media relevance 48 NA
Share on social media action 61 NA
Want to read more control 48 NA
Want to read more relevance 51 NA
Want to read more action 52 NA

Distributions by outcome

for (var in outcome_vars) {
  plot_data <- d |> filter(!is.na(.data[[var]]), !is.na(condition))
  if (nrow(plot_data) == 0) next
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = .data[[var]], fill = condition)) +
    geom_histogram(bins = 15, alpha = 0.8, position = "identity") +
    facet_wrap(~condition, ncol = 3) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = condition_colors, drop = FALSE) +
    labs(title = outcome_labels[[var]], x = NULL, y = "Count") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
  print(p)
}

Means plot

means_df <- map_dfr(outcome_vars, function(var) {
  d |>
    group_by(condition) |>
    summarise(
      mean = mean(.data[[var]], na.rm = TRUE),
      se   = sd(.data[[var]], na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(sum(!is.na(.data[[var]]))),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) |>
    mutate(variable = outcome_labels[[var]])
}) |>
  filter(!is.na(mean), is.finite(mean)) |>
  mutate(variable = factor(variable, levels = unname(outcome_labels)))

ggplot(means_df, aes(x = condition, y = mean, fill = condition)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean - se, ymax = mean + se), width = 0.2) +
  facet_wrap(~variable, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = condition_colors) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Mean (±1 SE)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1))

Outcome correlations

cor_mat <- d |>
  select(all_of(outcome_vars)) |>
  cor(use = "pairwise.complete.obs") |>
  round(2)

cor_df <- as.data.frame(cor_mat) |>
  rownames_to_column("var1") |>
  pivot_longer(-var1, names_to = "var2", values_to = "r") |>
  mutate(
    var1 = factor(var1, levels = outcome_vars, labels = outcome_labels[outcome_vars]),
    var2 = factor(var2, levels = outcome_vars, labels = outcome_labels[outcome_vars])
  )

ggplot(cor_df, aes(x = var1, y = var2, fill = r)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = r), size = 2.5) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#990000", mid = "white", high = "#011F5B",
                       midpoint = 0, limits = c(-1, 1)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, fill = "r") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 9) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 40, hjust = 1))
